key_insight: A recurrent neural network can emulate laminated-core hysteresis and eddy-current behavior accurately enough to replace expensive reference material calculations inside finite element simulations.
engineering_relevance: This enables far faster and more practical magnetodynamic FEM analyses of electrical machines without restricting the model to simple saturation-only behavior.
method: Train an RNN surrogate on artificially generated magnetic field sequences representative of machine operating conditions.
Embed the surrogate in 2D magnetodynamic finite element simulations using a magnetic vector potential formulation.
Compare its predictions against a reference laminated-core model and an anhysteretic baseline.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports excellent agreement with the reference laminated-core model while keeping computational cost to about twice that of an anhysteretic simulation.
watch_item: The surrogate must be trained on sufficiently diverse field histories to generalize reliably across different FEM simulations and operating regimes.
paper_title: Accounting for Hysteresis and Eddy Currents in Finite Element Simulations of Ferromagnetic Laminated Cores using a Recurrent Neural Network
